meaning: n.<美俚>[贬]白人n.<美俚>白鬼子(黑人对白人的蔑称)
examples: But by the time he was a teenager, he was tearing up local honky-tonks.
He was often hailed as a master of the telecaster, sort of a honky tonk Eric Clapton.
To capture Mr. Owens's patented sound, Mr. Farrar enlisted two fiddle players for the "Honky Tonk" sessions.
